title: CI/CT DO tradecraft v1.1; SAT enumeration + 12 analyst templates (BLUF, ACH, ICD-203)

fields.vision: CI/CT DO tradecraft v1.1 is an unclassified method packet for converting a long-form counterterrorism and counterintelligence paper into analyst-ready tradecraft templates.
fields.mechanism: It combines Red Team, kill-chain, Diamond Model, ACH, ICD-203 language discipline, BLUF forms, and twelve analyst templates so operational tradecraft, analytic method, and public-safe portfolio framing remain distinct.
fields.source_utilization: Intelligence tradecraft, counterintelligence, and analytic method. Use source library intelligence titles as method authority and intelligence-domain stressors. Vision: Present intelligence pages as disciplined analytic-method and tradecraft demonstrations. Mechanism: Tie claims to collection, covert action, deception, interrogation, bias-control, and analytic-logic mechanisms. Plan: Prioritize Capstone, SATS, CI, RAG integrity, and analyst-assurance pages for close-reading. Status: source library identified as candidate scholarly support; direct quotation requires exact passage review. Remaining gap: Need method-passage extraction and claim-to-source linkage. Source pool: 60 deduplicated local title candidates; 63 catalogue support rows. Examples: Improving Intelligence Analysis; Leading Intelligence Analysis. Research design, evidence evaluation, forecasting, and judgment. Use source library method and reasoning sources as public method and source background. Vision: Make methodology pages legible as research-design, evidence-control, and interpretive-method systems. Mechanism: Extract argument, warrant, close reading, corpus method, reasoning, evidence, and analytic-logic mechanisms. Plan: Strengthen source governance, evidence workbench, capstone, and Nexus method pages. Status: source library identified as candidate scholarly support; direct quotation requires exact passage review. Remaining gap: Need exact methodological passages for claim-control upgrades. Source pool: 129 deduplicated local title candidates; 136 catalogue support rows. Examples: Argumentation; From Human Reasoning to Belief. source library scholarly-source routing with author correction. Raw filenames often append Nik_Murano and are not treated as authorship evidence. Catalogue rows are preferred for authors where available. This strengthens page vision, mechanism, source-use, plan...
fields.plan: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. Read order: (1) 05_Role_Mapping — pick lane; (2) 04_Execution_Plan — start 90-day sprint, ship Capstone #1 in 14 days; (3) 03_DO_Lexicon — controlled vocab; (4) compendium + methods as reference; (5) templates as copy-paste scaffolds. "Done" = BLUF + probability + confidence rationale + alternatives + indicators + provenance appendix on every work product. Wire or preserve the implementation files: No executable code; work product set is markdown templates + two CSVs. Referenced tool stack: Gephi, NetworkX, QGIS, Neo4j, MITRE ATT&CK Navigator, MISP/OpenCTI.
fields.remaining_gaps: Promote the packet from documented handoff to live implementation only after the declared caveats are resolved: apply or wire the scaffold, run the stated validation or smoke-test sequence, and keep unresolved assumptions visible.
fields.detailed_source_vision: An unclassified, resume-safe, portfolio-oriented packet that converts a long "list of analytic techniques" into a usable system spanning three hiring lanes: CI/CT analytic tradecraft (structured analysis, warning, adversary modeling), DO-adjacent competence signaling (language/cultural/influence/judgment), and DA/Targeting/CTI readiness (rigorous products, tools, evaluation). Explicit pitch is that recruiters do not hire on framework name-drops — they hire on shipped work products. The packet forces the user to pick a primary lane and execute a 90-day plan with weekly sprints. Version 1.1 specifically: removed fabricated in-text citation markers from an earlier draft, added a real bibliography (SATs primer, ICD 203, Kill Chain, Diamond Model with URLs), and updated tool notes where availability changed (CrowdTangle shutdown — Meta Content Library pointer).
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: It converts an archive or handoff into three inspectable decisions: what the work product is for, which component pieces carry the work, and what action would turn it into a live room, paper, method, or software instrument.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: CI/CT DO tradecraft v1.1 is an unclassified method packet for converting a long-form counterterrorism and counterintelligence paper into analyst-ready tradecraft templates.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: It combines Red Team, kill-chain, Diamond Model, ACH, ICD-203 language discipline, BLUF forms, and twelve analyst templates so operational tradecraft, analytic method, and public-safe portfolio framing remain distinct.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It matters because CI/CT material has to be useful without becoming sensitive: the packet shows how to turn doctrine into professional templates while preserving source, role, and release boundaries.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: rendered correction table.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.

researcher_process_map.final_use_case: It matters because CI/CT material has to be useful without becoming sensitive: the packet shows how to turn doctrine into professional templates while preserving source, role, and release boundaries.
